sanskrit: तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत। सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः॥
transliteration: tam uvāca hṛṣīkeśaḥ prahasann iva bhārata senayor ubhayor madhye viṣīdantam idaṁ vacaḥ
literal_meaning: To him spoke Hṛṣīkeśa, smiling, O Bhārata, between the two armies, to the grieving one these words.
contextual_meaning: At the precise moment when Arjuna’s ego collapses into helpless grief, Kṛṣṇa responds with a gentle, almost playful smile—signaling that despair is the doorway to deeper inquiry. The dialogue is intentionally public, turning a private breakdown into a universal lesson on transforming paralysis into purposeful action.
psychological_state: Acute stress reaction with moral injury; ego disintegration opening space for new internal working models.
trigger_scenario: A team-leader freezes before announcing mass layoffs; the mentor steps in, calmly signaling, ‘We’ll walk through this together—right here, in front of everyone.’
anchor_text: When despair strikes, let inquiry begin and paralysis turn to purpose.
